# Chapter 20: Building a Context Engineering Practice

This chapter addresses how to **build a practice** — the people, processes, and culture to make context engineering work at scale.

## Roles and Responsibilities

A context engineering practice needs defined roles:

```yaml
roles:
  - name: Context Engineer
    responsibility: Building and maintaining knowledge graphs
    skills: [ontology, graph databases, semantic modeling]
    
  - name: Prompt Engineer
    responsibility: Designing and optimizing prompts
    skills: [LLM mechanics, testing, iteration]
    
  - name: Validation Engineer
    responsibility: Building validation gates
    skills: [testing, monitoring, drift detection]
    
  - name: Governance Lead
    responsibility: Ensuring context quality
    skills: [process design, auditing, compliance]
```

### Role Interactions

```javascript
const roleInteractions = {
  "Context Engineer": {
    collaboratesWith: ["Prompt Engineer", "Validation Engineer"],
    reportsTo: "Governance Lead",
    owns: ["knowledge-graph", "vocabulary", "thesaurus"]
  },
  
  "Prompt Engineer": {
    collaboratesWith: ["Context Engineer", "Validation Engineer"],
    reportsTo: "Engineering Lead",
    owns: ["prompts", "skill-definitions"]
  },
  
  // ...
};
```

## Skill Development

Train teams on context engineering:

```javascript
const trainingCurriculum = {
  foundation: [
    "Context Engineering fundamentals",
    "Knowledge graph basics",
    "Token budget management"
  ],
  
  intermediate: [
    "Building knowledge graphs",
    "Validation gate design",
    "Drift detection"
  ],
  
  advanced: [
    "Multi-agent orchestration",
    "Enterprise governance",
    "Custom tool development"
  ]
};
```

## Measuring Success

Track practice effectiveness:

```javascript
const practiceMetrics = {
  // Efficiency
  contextRetrievalTime: "ms to retrieve relevant context",
  tokenUsage: "average tokens per query",
  
  // Quality
  validationPassRate: "percentage passing gates first time",
  hallucinationRate: "grounded vs ungrounded outputs",
  
  // Velocity
  featureDeliveryTime: "time from spec to deployed",
  iterationCount: "prompt/skill improvement cycles"
};

async function reportPracticeHealth() {
  const metrics = await measureMetrics(practiceMetrics);
  const score = calculateScore(metrics);
  
  return {
    score,
    metrics,
    recommendations: getRecommendations(score, metrics)
  };
}
```

## Summary

Building a practice requires:
- Defined roles with clear ownership
- Training curriculum for skill development
- Metrics to measure success

Key Sources: Zestic AI framework, engineering practice building
